data.title: Gold and silver plunge in sudden selloff as stock market rout triggers algo trading
data.description:
Gold and silver futures plummeted Thursday as strong U.S. labor data dimmed hopes of near-term Federal Reserve rate cuts, with algorithmic traders apparently exacerbating the sudden drop.

The plunge in precious metals came as the stock market fell sharply, as investors worried the artificial intelligence buildout might disrupt the business models of whole industries and hurt some companies' future earnings, triggering a rout across risk assets.

The swift drop in the metals - which did not have an obvious catalyst - likely was intensified by selling from commodity trading advisors using computer models to bet on price moves, Bloomberg strategist Michael Ball said.

"Metals are overall getting hit hard in a sudden air-pocket kind of move lower that feels more like systematic strategy selling, the kind of momentum-driven de-risking you often see from the CTA community when levels give way," Ball wrote.

Stop-selling was blamed by Fawad Razaqzada, market analyst at City Index and Forex.com.

"Due to previous heightened volatility, a lot of people would have placed their stops either below $5,000 or above the $5,100 level just to preserve their stop positions," Razaqzada said in a note. "Because of the downward move, those stops have been triggered below the $5,000 level, and that caused a cascading-like effect, causing prices to slump in a short period of time."

"The market has now cleared out a large pocket of downside liquidity, and the next move will depend on how price behaves around key technical levels," Razaqzada added.

Gold and silver prices fell initially after strong U.S. jobs data tempered near-term expectations for fresh interest rate cuts, as non-farm payrolls rose by 130K jobs in January following a downwardly revised 48K increase in December, the unemployment rate edged down to 4.3%, and initial jobless claims fell to 227K in the week ended February 7.

Investors will watch U.S. inflation data due on Friday for more cues on the Fed's monetary policy path.

Finally, some of the day's selloff in the metals likely came from typical profit-taking, as the most recent blistering rally was driven partly by speculative buying.

"For gold and silver, a great deal of trading remains sentiment and momentum driven. On days like these, they will struggle," Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen said in a note.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery closed -2.9% to $4,923.70/oz, and front-month Comex February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished -9.8% to $75.546/oz, in the largest one-day decline for both metals since January 30.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Avient outlines $555M–$585M adjusted EBITDA target for 2026 while expanding innovation and debt reduction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Avient Corporation (AVNT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Ashish Khandpur, President, CEO & Chairman of the Board, highlighted "strong execution by our teams with favorable mix and management's tight cost control led to 80 basis points of adjusted EBITDA margin expansion and a strong 14% adjusted EPS growth for the fourth quarter." He noted that adjusted EBITDA margins expanded year-over-year in each quarter of 2025, and organic sales in Q4 were down 0.8% but grew 1.9% as reported due to favorable foreign exchange impact.
  • Khandpur pointed to double-digit business growth in defense, health care, and telecom, with packaging demand "improved modestly, growing sales low single digits in the fourth quarter compared to being down low single digits in the third quarter." He said, "adjusted EBITDA finished at $545 million for 2025 with 3.5% year-over-year growth as reported. Adjusted EPS grew 6%, helped by lower interest expense and favorable foreign currencies."
  • Khandpur emphasized innovation as a strategic driver, citing new non-PFAS products for catheters and packaging, along with process innovation in Dyneema fiber, designed to unlock new capacity to meet anticipated defense growth. "We plan to deploy incremental capital over the next 2 years to further expand capacity and support the growth we foresee from our Dyneema-based businesses."
  • Khandpur reflected on the company's evolved strategy since 2024, prioritizing organic growth, targeted M&A, profitability, and elimination of structural complexity. "Over the last 2 years, we have grown adjusted earnings per share by about 20% and expanded adjusted EBITDA margins by 70 basis points."
  • He stated, "Our strong free cash flow generation and disciplined capital deployment also allowed us to reduce debt, bringing net leverage down from 3.1x in 2023 to 2.6x in 2025."
  • Jamie Beggs, Senior VP & CFO, explained, "Continued strength in health care and improving packaging demand was not enough to offset demand conditions in consumer, industrial and building and construction, which led to a 3% decline in organic sales for the segment during the quarter. EBITDA margins declined 10 basis points as productivity initiatives helped mitigate the impact of inflation and reduced demand."
  • Beggs added, "Specialty Engineered Materials organic sales increased 3% as strong growth in defense, health care and telecommunications more than offset lower sales in energy, industrial and building and construction end markets." She highlighted 10% EBITDA growth in this segment for Q4.

Outlook

  • Beggs stated, "Our full year guidance reflects a balance between encouraging demand trends across our portfolio and continued macro uncertainty and volatility." She set full year 2026 guidance for adjusted EBITDA of $555 million to $585 million and adjusted EPS of $2.93 to $3.17. First quarter adjusted EPS outlook is $0.81.
  • She also outlined anticipated free cash flow of $200 million to $220 million for 2026, with capital expenditures of $140 million, primarily to support growth in the defense business. "Productivity will again play a role in supporting earnings growth and margin expansion in 2026."

Financial Results

  • Khandpur reported $545 million in adjusted EBITDA for 2025, 3.5% year-over-year growth, and record-high adjusted EBITDA margins of 16.7%. Adjusted EPS increased 6% for the year, and free cash flow reached $195 million. The company reduced outstanding debt by $150 million, ending the year with a net leverage ratio of 2.6x.
  • Beggs stated, "CAI organic sales declined 2%. Steady growth in health care throughout 2025 helped offset softer demand in consumer, industrial, transportation and building and construction. Packaging remained relatively resilient, ending the year flat versus 2024."
  • Specialty Engineered Materials organic sales grew 2%, with EBITDA margins declining 40 basis points due to planned maintenance and strategic investments.

Q&A

  • Michael Sison, Wells Fargo: Are you seeing green shoots in consumer, industrial, transportation, and construction areas? What improvement is needed to hit midpoint guidance? Khandpur: Part of the improvement is expected in Q1 for consumer and packaging in the U.S. "We are seeing better conditions than we were seeing before on the packaging side. As you saw that in Q4 itself, packaging was up 1% in the United States." On the guidance midpoint, "that assumes that there is a modest growth in all of there, probably low single-digit kind of growth and packaging also a little bit more modest growth."
  • Sison: How much growth in 2026 from innovation? Khandpur: Patent filings doubled over two years to 50-plus. "From a financial perspective, if you take 2025, our growth vectors grew high single digits."
  • Daniel Rizzo, Jefferies: Outlook for transportation? Khandpur: "Transportation for us... was overall down minus 1% for the year. In United States, the market was down minus 1% for the year, but we were down minus 5%." Q1 likely softer in Asia, with full year projection as "flattish to low single digit."
  • Rizzo: Should we expect more internal growth or M&A? Khandpur: "Probably not this year, maybe something after that when we also have a better balance sheet situation."
  • Frank Mitsch, Fermium: Debt paydown vs. buybacks? Khandpur: "In the near term, you can expect us to keep paying the debt versus buyback."
  • Unknown Analyst, KeyBanc: Productivity gains and new actions? Beggs: "We accomplished a little over $40 million of net productivity from last year. We expect about half of that to basically continue on into 2026."
  • Kristen Owen, Oppenheimer: Progress toward 20% EBITDA margin? Beggs: "Our goal is to get above 20% EBITDA margins... the last couple of years has been more focused on the mix dynamic and the productivity dynamic."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on macro improvement questions, innovation, and capital allocation, frequently pressing for specifics on market recovery and margin expansion. The sentiment was neutral to slightly positive, with cautious optimism.
  • Management maintained a confident yet cautious outlook, frequently referencing "cautiously optimistic" and noting both improvements and ongoing volatility. During Q&A, management provided detailed, data-driven responses, often reiterating the need for productivity and innovation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from defensive and contingency-focused to more optimistic, supported by recent margin and innovation gains. Analysts appeared more constructive and less skeptical than in the previous quarter, with fewer questions on downside risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more specific and optimistic, projecting adjusted EBITDA growth to $555 million–$585 million and adjusted EPS of $2.93–$3.17, compared to the prior quarter’s guidance range of $540 million–$550 million EBITDA and $2.77–$2.87 EPS.
  • Strategic focus continues on innovation and organic growth, with clear examples of recent product launches and process advancements. The previous quarter’s emphasis on contingency planning has given way to detailed capital deployment and capacity expansion plans.
  • Analysts’ focus has shifted from inventory and demand risks to questions about margin progression and innovation-driven growth.
  • Management’s confidence has grown, as evidenced by repeated references to record margin performance, debt reduction, and innovation pipeline progress.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing macro volatility, trade policy uncertainties, and geopolitical risks as potential headwinds for 2026.
  • Beggs described $30 million net inflation as a baseline challenge, with the company prepared to scale productivity initiatives based on demand. The CFO noted, "uncertainty remains with evolving global trade, labor market, GDP growth rates and foreign currency fluctuations."
  • Analysts questioned improvements in end markets and the sustainability of innovation-driven growth, but management reiterated preparedness with cost and productivity levers if demand weakens.

Final Takeaway

Avient’s leadership reported record margins and consistent earnings growth for 2025, supported by innovation, disciplined capital deployment, and targeted productivity initiatives. The company projects another year of earnings expansion in 2026, with adjusted EBITDA guidance of $555 million to $585 million and a focus on continued debt reduction, capacity investments in defense, and scaling growth vectors through new differentiated products. Management maintains cautious optimism amid lingering macro uncertainties, emphasizing readiness to drive profitability and margin expansion regardless of market conditions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: PTC Therapeutics drops as it pulls Translarna NDA resubmission
data.description:
• PTC Therapeutics (PTCT) is down ~5% in after-hours trading Thursday after announcing it has withdrawn its NDA resubmission of Translarna (ataluren) for nonsense mutation Duchenne muscular dystrophy.
  • The company made the decision based on US FDA feedback on its application review.
  • CEO Matthew Klein said that the data included in the application is "unlikely to meet the Agency's threshold of substantial evidence of effectiveness to support approval."
  • The drug is approved in the European Union.

data.title: Concentrix prices $600 million senior notes offering
data.description:
• Concentrix (CNXC) on Thursday said it has priced a public offering of $600 million aggregate principal amount of 6.500% Senior Notes due 2029.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering, together with other available funds, as necessary, to redeem or otherwise repay at or prior to maturity all or a portion of its 6.650% Senior Notes due August 2, 2026, of which $800 million aggregate principal amount is outstanding as of the date hereof, and pay related fees and expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 24, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DexCom Q4 results beat on both lines; 2026 revenue guidance includes consensus
data.description:
• DexCom's (DXCM) Q4 2025 financial results beat on both lines and the company reiterated its 2026 guidance range, which includes the consensus.
  • The maker of continuous glucose monitors sees revenue this year of $5.16B-$5.25B. Consensus is $5.23B.
  • Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 compares to $0.45 in the year-ago period. 
  • DexCom was helped in the quarter by a 13% year-over-year revenue increase to $1.26B. US revenue rose by 11%, while international revenue grew 18%.
  • The company ended 2025 with cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of $2B compared to $2.58B on Dec. 31, 2024.

data.title: H&R REIT reports Q4 results
data.description:
• H&R REIT press release (HRUFF): Q4 FFO per basic and diluted unit of C$0.312.
  • Rentals from investment properties of C$203.75M vs. C$202.35M a year ago.

data.title: Citi boosts CEO Fraser's 2025 pay by 22% to $42M
data.description:
Citigroup (C) increased Chair and CEO Jane Fraser's pay for 2025 to $42M, with 82% of the compensation tied to stock or performance share units, from $334.5M in 2024, according to a filing on Thursday.

The board cited Fraser's accomplishments, including record 2025 revenue in each of the bank's five core businesses, net income rising 13% Y/Y, progress in regulatory compliance, and reaching key milestones in its simplification strategy, among others.

The compensation consisted of $1.5B base salary, a $6.075M cash award, $14.175M in deferred stock, which vests over four years, and $20.25M in performance share units, which vest over three years.

data.title: Twilio in charts: Active customer accounts rise ~24% in Q4
Lululemons? Company hit with new complaints over quality of yoga pants
data.description:
Quality concerns are once again dogging lululemon athletica (LULU), with customer complaints over its “Heart Scatter” pants surfacing just weeks after backlash tied to the “Get Low” line.

“How do you make black leggings see thru. FAIL,” was one post on Reddit concerning the most recent line of yoga pants that are see-through when bending or squatting.

Last month, the company faced a barrage of complaints that the “Get Low” pants were see-through. Lululemon (LULU) briefly pulled the line from its website but placed them back online and advised customers to “size up” and wear flesh-colored undergarments.

“To experience the tights’ best performance during your workout, we recommend sizing up and wear skin-tone, seamless underwear,” lululemon posted on its website.

Is the third time the charm?

This most recent issue with the transparency of its yoga pants is the third occasion the quality of its products has come under fire. In 2013, the company paused the sale of its Luon pants over transparency issues, forcing it to issue full refunds or exchanges.

And in July 2024, lululemon (LULU) pulled its Breezethrough collection amid complaints of an unflattering design and poor fit.

The consecutive quality issues surface at a time when the company is already struggling to regain relevance with Gen Z and Millennials amid fierce competition. And its woes have been recently compounded by  the lack of a CEO, emergence of an activist investor who is seeking changes in the board, and a 57% erosion in the stock’s price year-over-year.

Fortunately, upbeat holiday sales encouraged the company to see Q4 revenue and earnings at the high end of its previously guided ranges of $3.50B to $3.58B and $4.66 to $4.76 per share, respectively, giving investors hope that the worst may be behind the Canadian apparel manufacturer.  

"We remain focused on executing our action plan to drive improvement in our U.S. business and look forward to the opportunities in front of us," said CFO Meghan Frank at the ICR Conference last month.

lululemon athletica has not yet responded to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Waste Connections targets $9.9B–$9.95B 2026 revenue as AI and acquisition gains bolster margin outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Waste Connections (WCN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Ronald Mittelstaedt, Founder, CEO, President & Director, highlighted, "Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 110 basis points in Q4, capping a strong year for Waste Connections, driven by price-led organic growth, solid waste and continued operating improvements." He also noted an industry-leading adjusted EBITDA margin of 33% for the year and $330 million of acquired annualized revenue.
  • Mittelstaedt emphasized, "We returned over $830 million to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends while preserving flexibility for continued growth and return of capital." He added that employee turnover and safety incident rates reached multiyear lows, stating, "In 2025, we reached historic company record levels in safety, our most important and impactful operating value."
  • The company closed 19 acquisitions in 2025, totaling $330 million in annualized revenue, with a 2026 rollover revenue contribution of approximately $125 million. Mittelstaedt stated, "Our focus has been and will continue to be solid waste, and we look forward to building on a model that has consistently delivered value creation."
  • Mittelstaedt described the ongoing closure and remediation process at Chiquita Canyon landfill, noting, "Political challenges of resolving this situation continue to exceed our updated expectations primarily because of related regulatory, permitting, legal, consulting and other unanticipated requirements."
  • Significant investments in renewable natural gas (RNG) and AI-driven operational initiatives were detailed, with the CEO citing, "We're just getting started and are already seeing positive outcomes as we expand the utilization of AI and data analytics across multiple platforms."
  • CFO Mary Whitney stated, "In the fourth quarter, we delivered revenue of $2.373 billion. Acquisitions completed since the year ago period contributed about $58 million of revenue in Q4, net of divestitures."

Outlook

  • Revenue for 2026 is estimated in the range of $9.9 billion to $9.950 billion. Whitney explained, "For solid waste collection, hauling and disposal, we expect organic growth in the range of 3.5% to 4%, driven by core pricing of 5% to 5.5% with expected yields of approximately 4%, implying volumes flat to down about 0.5 percentage point."
  • Acquisition revenue contribution of about $125 million reflects deals closed to date, while adjusted EBITDA in 2026 is expected in the range of $3.30 billion to $3.325 billion. Adjusted EBITDA margin is anticipated in the range of 33.3% to 33.4%.
  • Adjusted free cash flow is projected to increase by double-digit percentages to a range of $1.4 billion to $1.45 billion, with capital expenditures estimated at $1.25 billion, including $100 million for RNG and recycling projects. Closure-related outlays at Chiquita Canyon are expected to impact adjusted free cash flow by $100 million to $150 million.

Financial Results

  • Whitney reported, "Adjusted EBITDA for Q4, as reconciled in our earnings release, was up 8.7% year-over-year to $796 million or 33.5% of revenue, up 110 basis points year-over-year."
  • Full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA was $3.125 billion, with a margin of 33%. Adjusted free cash flow for 2025 was $1.26 billion, reflecting underlying conversion of adjusted EBITDA of approximately 50%. Capital expenditures for the year were $1.194 billion.
  • Whitney noted, "Q4 pricing accelerated sequentially to 6.4%... Reported volume down 2.7% was in line with prior quarters."

Q&A

  • Sabahat Khan, RBC Capital Markets, asked about sustainability CapEx and Chiquita outlays. Whitney answered, "We'd expect them both to step down '26 to '27... The $100 million includes the final $75 million... on RNG facilities... incremental $25 million... is part of our efforts longer term... to really derisk recycling."
  • Tami Zakaria, JPMorgan Chase & Co, inquired about expense moderation. Mittelstaedt responded, "We began 2025 with labor rates approaching 5% year-over-year, and we exited Q4 with labor rates up about 3.9% year-over-year and trending down towards 3% to 3.5% throughout 2026."
  • Noah Kaye, Oppenheimer & Co., asked about the M&A pipeline and free cash flow conversion. Mittelstaedt replied, "No reason to expect that '26 looks any different... There's nothing that has changed in the underlying opportunity basket... we certainly have the capacity... to do both whatever comes along at M&A and as much buyback and return of capital as we think is prudent."
  • Jerry Revich, Wells Fargo, asked about Northeast corridor build-out. Mittelstaedt explained, "We're now doing 7,500 tons a day sort of at the peak period... as we come through '26, we will be in the 9,000 to 9,500 tons a day into our Arrowhead Landfill."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but repeatedly pressed for clarity on the sustainability of free cash flow gains, Chiquita-related costs, and M&A pipeline strength. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on risk factors and operational execution.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, using phrases such as "we're extremely proud of our accomplishments" and "we are very confident in that $100 million... being done in '26 and then the contribution being there for '27."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remains confident, but with increased discussion of risk mitigation and operational headwinds, particularly regarding Chiquita and commodity prices.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 revenue, margin, and free cash flow is more specific and forward-looking than preliminary comments in Q3, now including a $9.9 billion to $9.950 billion revenue range and adjusted EBITDA of up to $3.325 billion.
  • Margin expansion continues, though the pace is moderating as cost pressures ease. In Q3, management emphasized overcoming headwinds and a normalized outlook, while Q4 commentary highlighted tangible operational improvements and further AI integration.
  • Analysts' focus has shifted slightly toward sustainability of cash flows and the impact of ongoing and new investments in technology, with less emphasis on immediate macroeconomic headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory delays and political challenges at Chiquita Canyon landfill continue to impact costs and timelines, with management noting, "Political challenges... continue to exceed our updated expectations."
  • Commodity pricing, particularly for recycled materials and RNG credits, remains a headwind with no material improvement assumed in 2026 guidance.
  • Weather and regional construction activity pose episodic risks but are not expected to materially impact the overall outlook.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that Waste Connections delivered record safety and retention results, expanded margins, and maintained strong acquisition momentum in 2025, setting the stage for $9.9 billion to $9.950 billion in 2026 revenue. Investments in AI and operational initiatives are already yielding productivity gains, while ongoing challenges at Chiquita Canyon and headwinds from commodity prices are being actively managed. The company projects another year of margin expansion and double-digit free cash flow growth, with upside potential from improved economic conditions, commodity values, and additional M&A activity.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
